title: Repair native Hermes Slack gateway migration gaps
description: Slack gateway broke after the native Rust Hermes migration because stale launch paths still invoke the removed hermes command, some hosts have orphan duplicate gateway processes, and resumed Hermes conversations can send invalid provider payloads ending with an assistant message.
acceptance_criteria: Install/restart docs and scripts use acc-agent hermes --gateway; ad-hoc interactive host debugging has an explicit acc-agent hermes --chat/--repl path; resumed conversations always end with a user turn before provider calls; tests pass; fleet gateways are redeployed and stale legacy processes are gone.
notes: Code fix committed and pushed as 28e749b: native acc-agent hermes --chat added, legacy hermes script paths updated, resumed conversation assistant-prefill bug fixed, fleet redeployed. Boris was later found crash-looping gateway due host DNS, not gateway code: /etc/systemd/resolved.conf.d/99-bullwinkle-dns.conf forced 100.100.100.100 while Tailscale accept-dns=false, causing Slack DNS SERVFAIL. Manual Boris repair applied and Slack auth.test passed; gateway process observed running. Remaining work filed separately: ACC-8wq, ACC-206, ACC-hp9, ACC-r1u, ACC-udz.
close_reason: Native Hermes Slack migration gaps repaired and deployed: legacy hermes launch paths replaced with acc-agent hermes --gateway/--chat, resumed conversation provider prefill issue fixed, stale gateway process cleanup added, fleet restarted at 0a7658c, gateway child health is visible, read-only fleet Slack gateway health passes, and no stale legacy gateway processes remain.

title: Remove orphaned hermes-agent gitlink
description: The repo no longer has a configured hermes-agent submodule, but HEAD still tracked hermes-agent as a gitlink (mode 160000) while .gitmodules was empty. Remove the orphaned gitlink and empty .gitmodules, stop active install/docs paths from implying a vendored Python hermes-agent, and restore a native hermes compatibility command backed by acc-agent hermes for direct host debugging.
acceptance_criteria: git ls-files --stage hermes-agent .gitmodules returns no tracked entries; git submodule status is empty; active docs/scripts no longer imply hermes-agent is vendored as a submodule or installed from Python; hermes --version and acc-agent hermes --version work from the Rust runtime; git status is clean after commit and push.
close_reason: Removed orphaned .gitmodules and hermes-agent gitlink; active setup/bootstrap/restart paths no longer install Python hermes-agent; native Rust acc-agent now supports hermes symlink invocation with --version/--help; install/restart paths create ~/.acc/bin/hermes; tests and smoke checks pass.

title: Complete Slack end-to-end agent smoke test
description: Code and deployment fixes are in place and Boris recovered to a running gateway after DNS repair, but the user interrupted the final live log check before a full end-to-end Slack response test. Because posting to Slack is externally visible, this needs explicit operator approval or a documented test window. Verify each expected Slack bot responds in the right workspace without provider assistant-prefill errors, session corruption, duplicate replies, or stale legacy Hermes processes.
acceptance_criteria: With operator approval, send or trigger a minimal Slack smoke test for expected agents/workspaces; confirm each response is produced by native acc-agent hermes --gateway; confirm no assistant-prefill provider errors in logs; confirm no duplicate gateway processes remain; record results in the issue notes.
notes:
2026-04-30 verification: fleet gateways are healthy at 0a7658c and read-only deploy/slack-gateway-health.sh passes for rocky, boris, natasha, and bullwinkle. Six Slack channel smoke messages plus two user-auth/as_user attempts were posted through Slack API and reached /api/chains with exact thread timestamps, proving Socket Mode ingress and chain recording. They did not produce replies because Slack delivered them with bot_id/app-origin metadata (actor_kind=bot, user jordanhubbard via app token), and native gateway intentionally ignores bot-origin messages. Available app user tokens also post as app-origin bot messages, so this cannot validate human-origin response behavior. Remaining acceptance requires a real human-origin Slack mention/DM or a test token/scope that posts without bot_id; until then this is an external/manual validation gap, not a known gateway runtime failure.
2026-04-30 human-origin OMGJKH smoke test: user sent @Rocky #1, @Bullwinkle #2, @Natasha #3, and @Boris #4 at 03:05Z. Chain records show all four as actor_kind=human channel messages. Rocky and Boris produced bot_reply events. Bullwinkle and Natasha recorded only the human message and no reply/thinking event, indicating their gateways saw message events but did not enter the app_mention reply path. Implementing fallback mention detection for normal Slack message events that explicitly contain this bot's <@bot_user_id>, with in-memory de-dupe to avoid double replies when both message and app_mention arrive.
Correction from operator: all Slack smoke messages were human-origin. Offtera produced no bot replies; OMGJKH produced only Rocky and Boris replies. Root-cause in Rust gateway path: unlike the pre-conversion Python adapter in ~/Src/hermes-agent/gateway/platforms/slack.py, Rust required app_mention for channel replies and treated any bot_id metadata as non-actionable. Patch now mirrors the old behavior more closely: normal channel message events with an explicit <@this_bot> mention are actionable, and bot_id metadata no longer suppresses an explicit self-mention; an in-memory reply de-dupe prevents double replies when both message and app_mention events arrive.
2026-04-30 fix deployed: committed f18ef0b Handle Slack mention messages in gateway, pushed to origin/main, and restarted boris, rocky, natasha, and bullwinkle. All four agents verified at f18ef0b with hermes-rust 0.1.0. deploy/slack-gateway-health.sh passes after heartbeat refresh: OMGJKH default gateways running on rocky, boris, natasha, bullwinkle as configured; Offtera gateway processes running on natasha and bullwinkle. Remaining validation requires a fresh human-origin Slack smoke message after f18ef0b because pre-fix events cannot be replayed through the gateway.
2026-04-30 Rocky Offtera root cause/fix: Rocky was hub-mode, so acc-server.service used ~/.acc/acc.json to supervise workers and that file only contained the default gateway; no gateway-offtera process was configured. Rocky also had slack/offtera/rocky secrets in the hub store, but deploy/secrets-sync.sh did not map structured slack/<workspace>/<agent> keys into SLACK_APP_TOKEN_OFFTERA/SLACK_BOT_TOKEN_OFFTERA, and restart-agent skipped secrets-sync because the script was not executable. Fixed in commits 86995fe and 3b60503: structured Slack secrets sync to workspace env vars, acc-agent json accepts leading-dot script paths, hub restarts reconcile gateway-<workspace> into acc.json, and hub restart reloads acc-server.service. Deployed Rocky at 3b60503; ~/.acc/acc.json now has gateway and gateway-offtera, env has OFFTERA/OFTERRA app+bot vars, and deploy/slack-gateway-health.sh passes for Rocky with both default and Offtera gateways running.

title: Expose gateway child health in agent status
description: The supervisor log showed Boris' gateway child exiting every minute, but /api/agents still reported the agent as online at the current revision. Slack gateway health should be visible in the agent registry/heartbeat so operators can distinguish an online worker from a working Slack responder. Track gateway/default and gateway-offtera process state, restart count/backoff, last exit status, last log reason such as auth.test failed or no platforms configured, and last successful adapter start/socket connection.
acceptance_criteria: Agent heartbeat or registry status includes gateway child health by workspace; crash-looping or missing gateway children are visible from /api/agents or a related endpoint; tests cover serialization and supervisor status updates; dashboard/CLI consumers can see unhealthy Slack gateway state without SSH.
close_reason: Gateway child health is now included in supervisor heartbeat and surfaced through /api/agents plus /api/agents/:name/health, including hub-supervised Rocky overlay; tests cover serialization and API visibility; all agents report running gateway children at revision 0a7658c.

title: Add fleet Slack gateway health check
description: After the native Hermes gateway migration, agents could show online while Slack gateway children were missing, duplicated, or crash-looping. We need a repeatable non-posting health check that inspects each registered online agent over the hub registry and SSH, verifies expected acc-agent hermes --gateway processes per workspace, checks recent logs for Slack adapter started/socket mode connected, and flags auth.test failures, DNS failures, no-platform exits, and duplicate stale gateways. This should avoid sending Slack messages unless the operator explicitly chooses an end-to-end smoke test.
acceptance_criteria: A deploy/script command reports Slack gateway health for all online agents; it exits non-zero for crash loops, missing expected gateways, auth failures, DNS failures, or duplicate stale gateways; it does not post to Slack by default; docs describe the expected per-agent gateway ownership.
close_reason: Added deploy/slack-gateway-health.sh read-only fleet check; final run passed on rocky, boris, natasha, and bullwinkle with expected gateway process counts, Slack DNS OK, no legacy gateway processes, and registry gateway health green.

title: Persist Boris DNS and Tailscale repair
description: Boris Slack gateway was crash-looping because host DNS had a Bullwinkle-specific systemd-resolved drop-in forcing DNS=100.100.100.100 while Tailscale accept-dns was false. MagicDNS was reachable but not recursive for public names, so slack.com/api.slack.com returned SERVFAIL and Slack auth.test failed. Manual repair applied on Boris: tailscale set --accept-dns=false, disabled /etc/systemd/resolved.conf.d/99-bullwinkle-dns.conf, added /etc/systemd/resolved.conf.d/99-boris-dns.conf with NVIDIA DHCP resolvers 10.63.172.197 10.10.10.53 10.10.10.54, restarted systemd-resolved. Need codify this so setup/restart scripts and docs preserve host-specific DNS and never apply Bullwinkle DNS policy to Boris.
acceptance_criteria: Boris setup/runbook documents the DNS policy; installation/restart scripts include tailscale set --accept-dns=false before tailscale up; scripts do not install Bullwinkle DNS overrides on Boris; a verification command confirms slack.com and api.slack.com resolve on Boris.
close_reason: Persisted Boris DNS/Tailscale repair: deploy/fix-dns-boris.sh disables Tailscale DNS and removes the bad 100.100.100.100 resolved drop-in; docs/bootstrap notes require tailscale set --accept-dns=false before tailscale up; Boris resolves Slack DNS and gateway health passes.

title: Disable Tailscale DNS before agent Tailscale bring-up
description: Installation/bootstrap scripts that bring up Tailscale must run tailscale set --accept-dns=false first so Tailscale does not replace host DNS and break external dependency resolution.
acceptance_criteria: All repo scripts that invoke tailscale up set accept-dns=false immediately before bring-up; docs mention the rule; shell syntax checks pass.
close_reason: Updated container Tailscale setup to disable accept-dns before up, documented the rule, and refreshed tracked Boris workqueue guidance for Tailscale-primary hub access. Verified bash syntax, rustfmt check, and diff whitespace.

title: Idle supervisors should keep agent registry heartbeats fresh
description: Agent services can be active and on the latest revision while the hub marks idle agents offline because only upgrade/task/queue paths post heartbeats. Add a supervisor-level periodic heartbeat so idle agents remain visible and capacity telemetry stays current.
acceptance_criteria: acc-agent supervise posts periodic /api/heartbeat telemetry with revision, runtime, capacity, executor and session data; focused and workspace tests pass; fleet is redeployed and agents remain online.
close_reason: Added supervisor-owned periodic heartbeats with idle capacity telemetry and tests; cargo fmt/check, cargo test --workspace, and diff whitespace checks pass.

title: Investigate stale reported ACC revisions on online agents
description: Agent status check at 2026-04-29T06:55Z found current main/origin at d30dfa5. Natasha reports d30dfa5, but rocky reports 6f2c619, boris reports ba7ebcf, and bullwinkle reports 4c6be18 while all are online. Confirm whether ccc_version is stale telemetry or actual old checkout/runtime, then update or fix heartbeat reporting.
acceptance_criteria: All online agents either report current main revision or the dashboard clearly distinguishes runtime version from last-upgrade revision; stale agents are upgraded or documented with a valid reason.
close_reason: Normal heartbeats now refresh revision telemetry: legacy ccc_version mirrors workspace_revision, runtime_version is explicit, and server health/agent registry exposes both fields.

title: Restore signed remote exec health checks
description: Agent status check found /api/exec returns 500 with AGENTBUS_TOKEN not configured. This prevents signed remote health probes and update checks against registered agents, even though AgentBus service itself is online.
acceptance_criteria: Hub has AGENTBUS_TOKEN/SQUIRRELBUS_TOKEN configured consistently with agents; POST /api/exec with a read-only status probe succeeds and returns results from all online registered agents.
close_reason: Added exec signing/bus-auth fallback for CCC-era token names, mirrored agent config aliases, synced AgentBus secrets mapping, and covered the alias path with a server test.

title: Protect gateway session history endpoints with auth
description: Exploration found /api/sessions routes for gateway Slack/Telegram rolling prompt history do not call state.is_authed, while the SessionStore treats the hub copy as authoritative cross-agent conversation context. This exposes session keys and message transcripts to unauthenticated GET/PUT/DELETE callers on the API surface.
acceptance_criteria: GET/PUT/DELETE /api/sessions/:key and GET /api/sessions require bearer auth; acc-client SessionsApi sends auth consistently; tests assert unauthenticated requests return 401 and gateway still persists history.
close_reason: Added bearer auth guards to /api/sessions routes and integration tests for unauthorized access plus authenticated persistence.

title: Align acc-client memory API with Rust memory routes
description: Exploration found acc-client::MemoryApi still calls POST /api/memory/search and POST /api/memory/store, but the Rust server exposes /api/memory/recall, /api/memory/ingest, /api/memory/ingest/bulk, /api/memory/recent, /api/memory/context, and /api/vector/* instead. Any consumer using acc-client memory search/store will 404 until the client is updated or compatibility routes are restored.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryApi uses the current server routes or server provides compatibility aliases; tests cover search/ingest client calls against the Rust router.
close_reason: Updated acc-client MemoryApi to call /api/memory/recall, /api/memory/ingest, and /api/vector/upsert as appropriate; added router-backed client tests.

title: Reconcile outstanding Git branches
description: Multiple local and remote branches have drifted from main and need to be audited, merged where appropriate, and cleaned up so main is the canonical branch.
close_reason: Reconciled all outstanding local and remote branches into main; selected phase artifacts were ported and stale branches were marked reviewed.

title: Add executor readiness sanity check before using tmux-backed coding CLIs
description: Before the agent uses a tmux-backed Claude, Codex, or Cursor coding session, it must first launch the CLI and verify it can answer a known LLM-only question rather than merely executing a shell echo. Only executors that pass this readiness probe should be used for coding. If all CLI executors fail the sanity check, the agent must fall back directly to API-based coding and stop retry-spinning on broken CLIs.
acceptance_criteria: There is a shared readiness probe for Claude/Codex/Cursor tmux-backed sessions; the probe requires an LLM-originated answer to a known question, not a shell-only response; failed executors are marked unusable for routing; and when all CLI executors fail the probe, coding falls back to direct API calls without repeated retry loops.
notes: Implemented tmux-backed CLI readiness probing plus no-spin API fallback in the queue worker. Added temporary tmux launch/capture/send helpers, an LLM-only sanity question probe for Claude/Codex/Cursor, 10-minute probe caching to avoid retry loops, and direct API fallback when all coding CLIs are unavailable or fail the probe.

title: Rewrite dispatch to route by live executor/session readiness
description: Dispatch using online state, executor compatibility, auth readiness, free session slots, current load, and optional project/session affinity instead of legacy capability flags.
acceptance_criteria: Coding tasks prefer agents with ready CLI sessions, saturated agents are skipped when free session slots are exhausted, and dispatch tests reflect the new model.
close_reason: Dispatch selection now uses live executor readiness, idle CLI session affinity, task-capacity telemetry, session spawn capacity, assigned-session hard filtering, and regression tests for ready-session preference and saturated-agent skipping.

title: Publish session-aware heartbeat telemetry
description: Include executor and session readiness, free session slots, and per-session state in heartbeats, and keep long-running keepalives from dropping capacity fields.
acceptance_criteria: Heartbeats consistently include tasks_in_flight, estimated_free_slots, and session telemetry; keepalives preserve capacity state; and the server stores and serves the payload.
close_reason: Heartbeats now publish typed executor/session telemetry, task capacity defaults, revision/runtime fields, and server-side storage/serving coverage; long-running keepalives preserve explicit capacity values.

title: Replace Claude one-shot subprocess execution with persistent sessions
description: Replace execFile-based Claude coding execution as the default path and route Claude work through the shared persistent session adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Default Claude coding tasks use a persistent tmux-backed session, one-shot subprocess mode is fallback/debug only, and long-running tasks are no longer capped by the current JS timeout path.
notes: Replaced the queue worker's default one-shot Claude subprocess path with the shared persistent tmux-backed Claude session adapter. One-shot Claude remains available only via ACC_DEBUG_ONE_SHOT_CLAUDE=true. On session-path failure, the worker falls back once to API-based coding instead of retry-spinning.

title: Implement shared PTY/tmux session adapter
description: Build a reusable adapter that can target a named session, inject work, wait for idle, collect output, and support foreground or background jobs.
acceptance_criteria: The adapter supports send, wait, and capture for tmux-backed coding sessions, background execution works for long jobs, and completion detection is test-covered.
notes: Implemented a shared Rust tmux session adapter in agent/acc-agent/src/cli_tmux_adapter.rs and expanded agent/acc-agent/src/tmux.rs with buffer paste/session lifecycle helpers. The adapter can detect or create a persistent Claude tmux session, inject a task, poll for the idle prompt, and capture session output.

title: Add bounded session spawning and memory-aware admission control
description: Enforce per-agent and per-executor session limits, refuse spawn when RAM headroom is low, and expose free session slots and spawn denials as telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Agents enforce configured session-count ceilings, deny spawns when memory headroom is insufficient, and heartbeats expose free session slots plus denial reasons.
close_reason: Added registry-backed spawn admission for total and per-executor session ceilings, memory-pressure denial reasons, heartbeat capacity telemetry, and tmux adapter enforcement before creating new sessions.

title: Implement tmux-based discovery for supported coding CLIs
description: Discover active claude, codex, cursor, and opencode sessions from tmux and classify session identity, executor type, and coarse state.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery finds supported CLI sessions by tmux pane or process inspection, degrades cleanly when tmux is absent, and publishes discovered sessions into the registry.
close_reason: Tmux discovery is implemented for claude, codex, cursor, and opencode panes, handles missing tmux by returning an empty discovery snapshot, classifies session identity/state/auth, and feeds the persisted session registry refresh path; discovery/tmux tests pass.

title: Add a local session registry on each agent
description: Implement a persistent node-local registry for coding sessions, including executor type, session name, project binding, state, auth readiness, and timestamps.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can persist and reload local session state across restarts; discovered and agent-spawned sessions share one registry; other modules can query session state.
close_reason: Implemented node-local persistent session registry with disk reload, queryable heartbeat snapshots, spawned-session upsert into the shared registry, capacity recalculation, and registry persistence tests.

title: Update client and model types for executor/session registration
description: Add typed request and response models for executors and sessions and update clients to publish and consume the new shape during migration.
acceptance_criteria: Registration and heartbeat code use typed executor/session models, backward-compatible decoding stays in place during rollout, and integration tests cover old and new payloads.
close_reason: Implemented typed agent registration/capabilities models and acc-client methods for canonical executor/session/capacity payloads; added wiremock coverage for registration and capabilities update.

title: Implement canonical live agent registration schema
description: Add one canonical registration format with live executors[] and sessions[], normalize legacy capabilities payloads server-side, and stop relying on nonexistent API capability behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Server accepts and stores one canonical executor/session payload, legacy payloads normalize cleanly, and common paths avoid ad hoc Value parsing.
close_reason: Canonical live agent schema is implemented in agents.rs: executors/sessions/capacity are normalized from canonical and legacy payloads, with route tests covering register and capability update paths.

title: Clean up dispatch and worker logic for the new task semantics
description: Update dispatch and claim filters to use executor fields for capability matching and honor preferred_agent or assigned_agent separately.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch selects agents by executor compatibility, agent claim logic no longer skips valid work due to overloaded executor metadata, and tests reflect the new semantics.
notes: Exploration follow-up: acc-client ListTasksBuilder sends project filter as query key 'project', but acc-server list_tasks expects 'project_id'. The gateway acc_tasks_list tool also describes agent filter as assigned-agent, while server list_tasks filters q.agent against claimed_by only. Include these client/tool/server semantic mismatches in the dispatch cleanup.
close_reason: Cleaned task filtering semantics for durable tasks: acc-client now sends project_id, server accepts project/project_id aliases, agent filter covers claimed_by or assigned_agent, and tests cover the corrected client/server behavior.

title: Split executor choice from agent affinity in task schema
description: Keep preferred_executor and required_executors executor-type-only, add a separate agent-affinity field, and remove all remaining code paths that treat preferred_executor as an agent name.
acceptance_criteria: No code path uses preferred_executor to mean an agent name; review fanout uses the new affinity field; task docs define executor preference and agent affinity separately.
close_reason: Moved legacy queue peer affinity from preferred_executor to preferred_agent, added QueueItem preferred_agent, preserved dual-write metadata, and covered with queue/agent tests.

title: Epic: session-aware routing and capacity control
description: Route work by live executor readiness, session capacity, auth state, and load rather than legacy capability flags alone.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch prefers ready CLI sessions for coding work, skips saturated nodes, and uses session-aware telemetry consistently.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: persistent CLI executor adapters
description: Replace one-shot coding subprocess wrappers with persistent tmux/PTTY-backed adapters for supported coding CLIs.
acceptance_criteria: Claude, Codex, and optional Cursor executors can target long-lived sessions instead of short-lived subprocess timeouts.
close_reason: All persistent CLI executor adapter child tasks are closed: shared tmux/PTTY adapter, Claude/Codex/Cursor support, and readiness checks.

title: Epic: node-local coding session manager
description: Add node-local discovery, registry, health, and spawn policy for tmux/PTTY-backed coding sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can discover, track, and manage persistent coding sessions with health state, auth readiness, and capacity limits.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: canonical executor and session registration schema
description: Define one typed executor/session model for agent registration, heartbeats, and routing so server and agents can reason about real executor readiness instead of overloaded capability blobs.
acceptance_criteria: Server and clients use one canonical executor/session payload, with backward-compatible normalization of legacy registration shapes.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: orchestration unification for CLI-first task routing
description: Collapse orchestration onto /api/tasks, remove semantic overloads, and stop treating legacy queue and exec paths as peers to the canonical task plane.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture docs, schemas, and runtime behavior all treat /api/tasks as canonical durable work; legacy queue and exec paths are explicitly compatibility/operator-only.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: feat(hermes): native Rust gateway — Slack + Telegram parity with Python hermes-agent
description:
## Why this issue exists

The Python hermes-agent gateway was removed (CCC directive: Python-free fleet).
The fleet uses two platforms: Slack (Socket Mode) and Telegram. Mattermost is
retired. Both platforms are currently dark. This issue tracks the Rust rewrite.

## What needs to be done

Implement acc-agent hermes --gateway as a native Rust long-running process with:

### Core infrastructure
- GatewayConfig: load which platforms are enabled from env
- SessionStore: per (platform, channel_id, user_id) conversation history,
  persisted to disk at ~/.acc/data/sessions/
- GatewayRunner: spawn one task per enabled platform adapter, route incoming
  messages through HermesAgent::run_conversation(), deliver responses

### Slack adapter (Socket Mode)
- Connect via SLACK_APP_TOKEN (xapp-...) to Slack Socket Mode WebSocket
- Handle events: message, app_mention, slash commands
- Post replies via chat.postMessage (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN / SLACK_OMGJKH_TOKEN)
- Thread awareness: reply in thread if message was in a thread

### Telegram adapter
- Connect via TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN using long-polling (/getUpdates)
- Handle: private messages and group @mentions
- Post replies via sendMessage API
- Support basic formatting (markdown → Telegram markdown)

### HermesAgent integration
- Add run_conversation(history, message, context) method to HermesAgent
- Context: platform, channel_id, user_id, display_name
- Reuse existing provider chain and tool registry

## Acceptance criteria
- Slack DMs and @mentions to the bot produce AI responses
- Telegram private messages and @mentions produce AI responses
- Conversation history persists across messages in the same channel
- acc-agent hermes --gateway runs stably, supervised by acc-server
- No Python anywhere in the process tree

## Out of scope
- Mattermost (retired)
- Other 17 Python platform adapters
- PII redaction, session auto-reset, streaming/progressive edits (phase 2)
close_reason: Native Rust gateway implemented: Slack Socket Mode + Telegram long-polling, session persistence, run_gateway_turn, supervised by acc-server. Deployed and connected (confirmed live Slack WebSocket).

title: Eliminate JSON↔SQLite dual-mode: make SQLite the single source of truth for agent state
description:
Why: The agents registry, queue, secrets, and projects all live simultaneously in JSON files and SQLite, with a one-time migration that can diverge under concurrent writes or partial failures. This is the codebase's biggest coherence hole — the watchdog's offline detection, the dispatcher's routing, and the heartbeat table can all disagree about agent state.

What needs to be done:
- Audit all routes that read from or write to agents.json, queue.json, secrets.json, projects.json
- Replace all reads/writes with SQLite equivalents (fleet.db / auth.db)
- Delete the JSON fallback paths and the dual-mode migration toggle
- Consolidate the two heartbeat formats (JSONL and SQL table) into the SQL table only
- Ensure acc-agent migrate handles the one-time data lift for existing deployments
- Update tests to use SQLite only (no JSON fixtures)
close_reason: fleet_db (SQLite) is now the only durable store for agents, queue, secrets, projects. JSON migrate_from_json runs once at startup, then JSON files are never written again. 12 files changed, all tests green.

title: F6: sync state persistence across restarts
description: github-sync.py must persist last-synced timestamp per repo to ~/.acc/data/github-sync-state.json so restarts don't re-ingest already-seen issues. On first run, fetch all open issues. On subsequent runs, use 'since' parameter on gh API call. State file is JSON: {"owner/repo": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}. Idempotency guarantee: issue number is the dedup key, not timestamp.
close_reason: Implemented

title: F4: fleet task completion → GitHub comment/close
description: When a fleet task with metadata.github_number is marked completed, acc-server posts a comment to the linked GitHub issue via 'gh issue comment'. If GITHUB_AUTO_CLOSE=true, also closes the issue. Hook into routes/tasks.rs complete handler (or watchdog). Requires GITHUB_REPOS to be set so we know which repo to target. Configurable: GITHUB_AUTO_CLOSE (default false).
close_reason: Implemented

title: F2: triage gate — agent-ready label creates fleet task
description: Extend github-sync.py (F1): when a GitHub issue has the label matching GITHUB_DISPATCH_LABEL (default: agent-ready), call POST /api/tasks to create a fleet task. Fleet task stores metadata.beads_id and metadata.github_number. GH issues without the label are ingested into beads only — no fleet task. Configurable label name via env var.
close_reason: Implemented

title: F1: github-sync.py — poll GitHub issues into beads (inbound)
description: Write scripts/github-sync.py that polls 'gh issue list' for repos in GITHUB_REPOS env var. For each issue not yet in beads: bd create with source=github, github_number, github_repo, github_url, github_labels in metadata. For updated issues: bd update. For closed GH issues already open in beads: bd close. Idempotent — running twice must not create duplicates. Sync state (last-seen timestamp per repo) saved to ~/.acc/data/github-sync-state.json.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Two-way sync: GitHub issues ↔ beads ↔ fleet tasks
description:
External contributors opening GitHub issues never reach the agent fleet. Need a bidirectional sync pipeline:

1. GitHub → beads: poll or webhook-driven ingestion of new/updated GitHub issues into beads (already partially exists via /api/issues/sync but not automated or bidirectional)
2. beads → fleet: newly ingested beads issues should optionally create fleet tasks (triage step — not all GH issues become tasks)
3. fleet → beads/GH: when a fleet task closes, update the linked beads issue and optionally close the GH issue

The sync must handle: dedup (don't recreate existing), linking (beads id ↔ GH issue number ↔ fleet task id), status propagation, and external contributor attribution.

Design choices to resolve:
- Webhook vs. poll (webhook is real-time but requires public endpoint; poll is simpler)
- Auto-promote all GH issues to fleet tasks, or triage step (label/tag gating)
- Where sync state lives (beads metadata vs. acc-server DB)
close_reason: Completed the two-way pipeline: poll sync deduplicates GitHub issues into beads, dispatch-label issues create source=github fleet tasks, completed fleet tasks close linked beads and notify GitHub, opt-in mirror creates linked GitHub/beads entries, sync state persists, and runbooks/systemd timer document automation.

title: Detect and alert when agents stop work with open tasks
description: Agents sometimes abandon work sessions while open beads/tasks remain uncompleted. Need a mechanism to detect this — e.g. monitor agent heartbeats + cross-reference with claimed/in-progress beads across all projects. When an agent goes idle (no heartbeat or session activity for >N minutes) but has open claimed tasks, alert jkh. Could be a heartbeat check, a cron job, or built into ACC server.
close_reason: Watchdog already implements offline-agent claimed-task alerts; added behavioral coverage proving an abandoned-work bus alert and recent-alert record are emitted.

title: T11: idle agent detection
description: In dispatch tick, implement detect_idle_agents(). An agent is idle when: online (lastSeen <300s), online for >= ACC_IDLE_GRACE_PERIOD seconds, has 0 tasks with status in (claimed,in_progress), AND no open non-discovery/non-idea tasks exist that the agent is capable of claiming. Returns list of idle agent names.
close_reason: Closed

title: T12: discovery task auto-creation for idle agents
description: For each idle agent from T11: skip if agent already has an open discovery task. Find projects with zero open idea tasks, sorted by oldest last-activity. If no such project, skip. Create discovery task (priority=4, status=claimed, claimed_by=<agent>, metadata.auto_created=true). Publish tasks:dispatch_assigned to agent on bus.
close_reason: Closed

title: T10: Rocky response handler and expiry timeout
description: Subscribe dispatch loop to bus broadcast receiver for rocky:human_response messages. Handle actions: extend_7d (advance expiry +7d), promote_anyway (force-promote bypassing vote count), let_expire (mark rejected). Also: on each tick expire ideas where effective expiry < now AND rocky_warn_sent_at < now - ACC_ROCKY_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT.
close_reason: Closed

title: T9: Rocky pre-expiry warning via bus
description: In dispatch tick, call check_idea_expiry(). For open idea tasks where created_at < now - (ACC_IDEA_VOTE_EXPIRY - ACC_IDEA_EXPIRY_WARN_BEFORE) and metadata.expiry_warned != true: set metadata.expiry_warned=true and rocky_warn_sent_at=now, publish rocky:ask_human on bus_tx with idea summary and action choices: extend_7d, promote_anyway, let_expire.
close_reason: Closed

title: T8: idea tally, promotion, and rejection
description: In dispatch tick, call tally_idea_votes(). Count approve votes with non-empty refinements. If >= ACC_IDEA_APPROVE_THRESHOLD: create new work task with merged refinements appended, set metadata.promoted_from, mark idea status=completed. If rejections >= ACC_IDEA_REJECT_THRESHOLD: mark idea status=rejected. Publish tasks:added for promoted work task.
close_reason: Closed

title: T7: vote nudges in dispatch loop
description: In dispatch tick, scan open idea tasks with < ACC_IDEA_APPROVE_THRESHOLD approvals. For each, collect online agents who have not yet voted and are not the creator. Publish tasks:dispatch_nudge with to:<agent> per eligible agent. Rate-limit to once per tick per idea per agent.
close_reason: Closed

title: T6: PUT /api/tasks/:id/vote endpoint
description: Add vote endpoint to routes/tasks.rs. Validate: task exists, task_type=idea, refinement non-empty, agent != metadata.created_by. Upsert into metadata.votes array. Return updated task. Also patch create_task to set metadata.created_by=agent when task_type=idea. Routes: PUT /api/tasks/:id/vote.
close_reason: Closed

title: T5: tick loop — broadcast nudge, explicit assign, backfill
description: Implement full dispatch tick body: (1) Backfill — tasks older than ACC_DISPATCH_BACKFILL_THRESHOLD go straight to explicit assignment. (2) Broadcast nudge at 30s — publish tasks:dispatch_nudge to:null, update metadata.dispatch.last_nudge_at. (3) Explicit assign at 90s — atomic SQL claim for best agent, publish tasks:dispatch_assigned. Respect blocked_by and max assign attempts. Store dispatch state in metadata.dispatch JSON.
close_reason: Closed

title: T4: agent wakes poll loop on bus nudge
description: In agent/acc-agent/src/tasks.rs, spawn background task subscribing to agentbus SSE /bus/stream. On tasks:dispatch_nudge where to matches own name or to is null, signal main poll loop via tokio::sync::Notify to run immediately. Also wake on tasks:dispatch_assigned. Reconnect after 5s on SSE disconnect. Ignore nudge if at capacity.
close_reason: Closed

title: T3: directed nudge on task create
description: In routes/tasks.rs create_task, after tasks:added bus publish, call dispatch::nudge_task(). Helper calls select_best_agent, publishes tasks:dispatch_nudge with to:<agent-name> if capable agent online, else to:null broadcast. Existing tasks:added message must still fire.
close_reason: Closed

title: T2: capability matcher pure functions + unit tests
description: Implement select_best_agent(task, agents, claimed_counts) -> Option<String> in dispatch.rs as a pure function. Filter online agents by capability match, score by load, break ties alphabetically. Unit tests: no requirement, specific executor, least loaded, tiebreak, no eligible agents, blacklisted agent excluded.
close_reason: Closed

title: T1: dispatch.rs skeleton + tokio::spawn in main.rs
description: Create acc-server/src/dispatch.rs with pub async fn run(state: Arc<AppState>) tick loop. Wire into main.rs via tokio::spawn. Read all ACC_DISPATCH_* env vars with defaults. ACC_DISPATCH_ENABLED=false exits loop immediately. Expose pub mod dispatch in lib.rs.
close_reason: Closed

title: agents: register ssh_user/ssh_host/ssh_port in heartbeat
description: Agents must register their full SSH connection details (user, host, port) on every heartbeat so the hub registry is the authoritative source for reaching any agent. Currently only host is registered; username and port are unknown, causing guesswork (e.g. Boris uses 'horde' not 'jkh', port 26510 not 22). Add AGENT_SSH_USER/AGENT_SSH_HOST/AGENT_SSH_PORT to each agent .env, include in heartbeat payload across queue-worker.py/agent-pull.sh/acc-agent, and add to hub telemetry_keys allowlist.
close_reason: ssh_user/ssh_host/ssh_port added to all heartbeat senders and hub allowlist; AGENT_SSH_* written to each agent's .env

title: acc-server: add migration for fleet_tasks.phase column
description: acc-server panics on startup with 'no such column: phase' when trying to create idx_fleet_tasks_phase index. The fleet_tasks table predates the phase column. Need a proper schema migration so fresh and existing deployments both work without manual sqlite intervention.
close_reason: Fixed: removed idx_fleet_tasks_phase from init_schema, reordered migrations v<2→v<3→v<4, phase index now created idempotently in v4 migration

title: Investigate Boris task capacity stuck at maximum
description: While debugging the Slack gateway, Boris task logs repeatedly reported 'at work capacity (4/4), waiting' and review claim 429 errors. This may be unrelated to Slack, but it can make Boris appear online while unable to accept work. Investigate whether stale sessions/tasks are inflating capacity, whether the registry and task worker disagree about free slots, and whether cleanup is needed after restarts or failed task attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Boris task capacity matches actual running sessions/tasks after restart; stale sessions or task leases are cleaned up or explained; 429 review claim errors are understood; add a regression test or operational cleanup command if stale state was the cause.
close_reason: Boris capacity root cause fixed: server-dispatched tasks were moved directly to claimed, while acc-agent only polled open tasks and its work-capacity counter included reviews/phase_commit unlike the server. acc-agent now adopts assigned claimed/in_progress rows, guards duplicate local execution, and counts only work-equivalent task types for work capacity. Boris was redeployed at 0a7658c; startup released stale claims, assigned tasks were adopted/executed, and current Boris claimed/in_progress count is zero.

title: Fix supervisor health reporting for ACC child processes
description: Agent status check found /api/supervisor/status reporting PIDs for bus, queue, tasks, hermes, gateway, proxy, and slack-ingest, but every process has healthy=false. Hub /api/health and /api/services/status show acc-server/agentbus online, so either the child processes are actually unhealthy or the supervisor health semantics/config are wrong.
acceptance_criteria: Supervisor status accurately distinguishes running-with-no-healthcheck from unhealthy; configured child process health checks pass or expose clear failure reasons.
close_reason: Supervisor now reports health_status/health_reason/health_url and marks running children without health URLs as running_no_healthcheck instead of unhealthy; added route coverage.

title: Write migration and operator runbooks for CLI-first consolidation
description: Add a staged migration plan plus runbooks for stuck sessions, expired auth, memory pressure, spawn denials, and API fallback mode.
acceptance_criteria: Rollout phases are documented from schema compatibility through dispatch cutover, and operators have clear remediation steps for common failure modes.
close_reason: Added CLI-first migration runbook with rollout phases and operator remediation for stuck sessions, expired auth, memory pressure, spawn denials, executor limits, and API fallback.

title: Add compatibility freeze guards for legacy queue and exec paths
description: Prevent new feature work from landing on /api/queue, emit warnings or metrics when legacy paths are used, and document retention/removal conditions.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy path usage is visible in logs or metrics, and architectural docs treat the routes as compatibility-only.
close_reason: Legacy /api/queue and /api/exec routes now emit acc.compat warnings on use, and README retention criteria mark them compatibility-only until mixed-version usage stops.

title: Define and implement a minimal default agent runtime
description: Make the default runtime task worker plus session manager plus bus listener, while Hermes, gateways, proxy, and legacy queue remain optional.
acceptance_criteria: The default runtime no longer implies every node should run all worker classes, and startup docs separate required from optional processes.
close_reason: Supervisor default now starts only durable tasks and bus by default; legacy queue and Hermes polling require explicit opt-in flags, and startup docs separate minimal and optional processes.

title: Make API executors secondary for coding work
description: Change routing policy so coding tasks default to CLI executors while API providers remain useful for light reasoning, planning, summaries, and non-coding work.
acceptance_criteria: Default coding work is not routed to the in-process Anthropic loop when a ready CLI session exists, and docs describe API providers as secondary for coding.
close_reason: Default work-task execution now consults live session registry telemetry and prefers ready/spawnable CLI executors before falling back to the API agent loop; docs already mark API providers as secondary for coding.

title: Add persistent Codex session support
description: Support Codex as a persistent session-backed coding executor while preserving model and base-url overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Codex tasks can run via discovered or spawned persistent sessions and report auth readiness and busy state through the session registry.
notes: Implemented persistent Codex session support through the shared Rust tmux adapter. Queue-worker executor selection now routes codex_cli through a persistent tmux session after readiness probing, with optional ACC_DEBUG_ONE_SHOT_CODEX=true subprocess fallback and one-shot API fallback on session-path failure.

title: Add session health, stuck detection, and auth-readiness classification
description: Distinguish idle, busy, stuck, dead, and unauthenticated sessions using observable signals and configurable thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: Sessions transition between health states based on observable signals, unauthenticated sessions are distinct from dead sessions, and stuck sessions are identifiable without manual inspection.
close_reason: Session discovery now has explicit tested health classification for idle, busy, stuck, dead, and unauthenticated states using pane liveness, auth output, activity age, and configurable thresholds.

title: Declare /api/tasks as the canonical durable task plane
description: Update architecture and operator docs so /api/tasks is the only durable work plane, /api/queue is legacy compatibility only, and /api/exec is operator-only remote execution.
acceptance_criteria: Docs no longer describe /api/queue as primary scheduling, coding defaults to CLI sessions instead of API loops, and durable work is clearly separated from operator commands.
close_reason: Updated README runtime guidance and directives to make /api/tasks the durable work plane, /api/queue legacy ingress, and /api/exec operator-only.

title: Epic: migration safeguards and operator visibility
description: Roll out the consolidation safely with compatibility guards, dashboard visibility, and runbooks.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy-path usage is visible, operators can see session health/capacity, and rollout/runbook docs cover the common failure modes.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: minimal default agent runtime
description: Simplify the default node runtime to the smallest set needed for CLI-first task execution and bus coordination.
acceptance_criteria: The default runtime is task worker plus session manager plus bus listener, with other workers treated as optional add-ons.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Unified task model: merge /api/queue and /api/tasks into single DAG store
description: Currently the fleet has two separate task systems: the legacy work queue (/api/queue, /api/item/:id) and the DAG-backed fleet tasks (/api/tasks). This creates dual codepaths in the agent (queue_worker vs fleet_worker), inconsistent heartbeat mechanisms, and prevents queue items from participating in DAG dependency chains.\n\nWhat needs to be done: merge both into the fleet_tasks table with a backward-compatible adapter layer for legacy queue producers, migrate all agents to use /api/tasks exclusively, and retire the queue endpoints once all producers have migrated.
close_reason: Unified task model bridge implemented: migration v7 adds source column, POST /api/queue dual-writes to fleet_tasks with source=queue, GET /api/tasks?source=queue exposes them. Full lifecycle sync deferred — queue_items remain authoritative for lifecycle while fleet_tasks provide observability and DAG participation.

title: Formalize fleet task dependencies as a DAG scheduler with parallel execution planning
description:
Why: The fleet_tasks table has a blocked_by column but dependency resolution is ad-hoc in the dispatcher — agents individually poll for whatever happens to be unblocked. This works but loses the ability to reason about the execution graph, optimize scheduling (e.g. co-locate tasks with agents that already have the right workspace checked out), or express and visualize complex workflows like 'run these 8 tasks in parallel, merge results, then validate'.

What needs to be done:
- Model the dependency graph explicitly: task nodes + directed edges (blocks/depends-on), cycle detection on insert
- Add a scheduler that, when a task completes, computes which newly-unblocked tasks are now ready and emits work.available for each
- Add a parallel fanout primitive: a parent task that fans out N child tasks simultaneously and re-queues the parent's continuation when all children complete (join)
- Expose GET /api/tasks/graph returning the DAG as nodes+edges JSON for dashboard visualization
- Add capability hints to tasks at creation time (e.g. requires: [gpu, python]) so the scheduler can prefer agents that match rather than first-come-first-served
- Update the dispatch nudge to include capability requirements so agents self-select rather than claiming and failing
close_reason: DAG scheduler implemented: cycle detection, auto-unblock on complete, fanout primitive, graph endpoint, capability hints in nudge, agent self-selection

title: Replace polling + work-signal filesystem semaphore with SSE-driven work delivery
description:
Why: Agents currently poll /api/queue every N seconds and use a ~/.acc/work-signal filesystem semaphore for faster wake-up. This is two mechanisms solving one problem, adds 0–30s latency to task assignment, and makes it hard to reason about delivery guarantees. The AgentBus SSE stream already reaches every agent; completing that circuit eliminates polling entirely.

What needs to be done:
- Emit a work.available bus event (with item_id) when new work is posted to /api/queue or /api/tasks
- Update acc-agent bus subcommand to handle work.available: immediately attempt claim rather than waiting for next poll cycle
- Remove the filesystem semaphore (~/.acc/work-signal) write from the hub and read from the agent
- Collapse the three-phase dispatch escalation (immediate nudge → 30s broadcast → 90s force-claim) into a single push-on-post model with a single fallback timer for agents that miss the SSE event
- Verify delivery under SSE reconnection: agent must re-poll once on reconnect to catch any events missed during disconnect window
close_reason: SSE-driven work delivery implemented: queue worker now embeds its own bus subscriber with tokio::select! wakeup, filesystem work-signal eliminated

title: F7: beads metadata schema for GitHub-linked issues
description: Document and enforce the metadata shape for beads issues that have GitHub counterparts: {source, github_number, github_repo, github_url, fleet_task_id, github_labels}. Update bd create and bd update calls in github-sync.py to always populate this schema. Update /api/issues list/get to expose these fields. Write a migration script that back-fills existing linked issues.
close_reason: GitHub-linked beads now use structured metadata populated by github_sync create/update/migration paths, /api/issues exposes the schema, and tests cover metadata normalization.

title: F5: beads → GitHub mirror (opt-in)

Subsequent bd close should optionally close the GH issue. Bidirectional link: beads has github_number; GH issue body references ACC-xxx.

title: F3: POST /api/github/webhook — real-time GitHub event handler

Handle events: issues.opened, issues.labeled, issues.closed, issues.edited. On labeled with agent-ready: same fleet task creation as F2. On closed: propagate to beads. Invalid HMAC → 401. Unit tests for HMAC validation and event routing.

No corresponding GitHub issue found — delivered directly as an agent task. Pipeline appears to be functioning: task was received, environment inspected, and this tracking issue created.

title: Add dashboard visibility for sessions and capacity
description: Show per-agent session lists, executor readiness, stuck/dead state, and free capacity in the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Operators can see which CLI sessions exist and whether they are usable, and saturated or stuck agents are visible without logging into the node.
close_reason: Agent dashboard cards now show task/session capacity, spawn denial reasons, executor readiness, and per-session state with stuck/dead/unauthenticated highlighting; UI test covers the visibility hooks.

title: Demote GPU and vLLM from baseline scheduling assumptions
description: Treat GPU and vLLM executors as optional classes rather than part of the default operating model.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline docs no longer imply GPU or vLLM is a required/default coding path, and routing defaults do not assume local model-serving capacity exists.
close_reason: Updated stale executor design guidance so vLLM is explicit opt-in only, and added dispatch coverage proving generic work prefers ready CLI sessions over GPU/vLLM-only capability.

title: Add persistent Cursor session support
description: Support Cursor as a persistent session-backed coding executor and keep it opt-in until it is stable.
acceptance_criteria: Cursor tasks can target a persistent session when explicitly requested and Cursor auth/session readiness is exposed in registration and telemetry.
notes: Implemented persistent Cursor session support through the shared Rust tmux adapter. Queue-worker executor selection now routes cursor_cli through a persistent tmux session after readiness probing, with optional ACC_DEBUG_ONE_SHOT_CURSOR=true subprocess fallback and one-shot API fallback on session-path failure.

title: Complete queue→fleet lifecycle sync (full unified task model)
description: The bridge from ACC-y9n was implemented: queue items are dual-written to fleet_tasks when created. But the lifecycle (claim/complete/fail via /api/item/:id/*) is NOT yet mirrored back to fleet_tasks. A queue item claimed via the legacy endpoint won't show as 'claimed' in fleet_tasks.\n\nWhat needs doing: (1) When /api/item/:id/claim is called, also update fleet_task status to 'claimed'. (2) When /api/item/:id/complete is called, also complete the fleet_task with output. (3) When /api/item/:id/fail, also fail the fleet_task. (4) Optionally: agent poll_queue switches from /api/queue to /api/tasks?source=queue so it uses fleet task lifecycle natively.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 9b8f850: db_fleet_sync_claim/complete/fail/keepalive helpers added to db.rs; all four queue handlers (claim, complete, fail, keepalive) now mirror state to fleet_tasks WHERE source='queue'.

title: Add server-side tests for capability registry and DAG result propagation
description: The four architectural improvements (capability registry, keepalive reclamation, result propagation, conversation persistence) were implemented but the server-side changes in acc-server/src/routes/agents.rs, tasks.rs, and db.rs lack dedicated integration tests.\n\nNeeded: tests that verify (1) PUT /api/agents/:name/capabilities stores capabilities correctly and select_best_agent filters by requires[], (2) PUT /api/tasks/:id/keepalive extends claim_expires_at, (3) completing a task with output= populates inputs on unblocked children, (4) GET/POST /api/tasks/:id/turns round-trip correctly.
close_reason: Added server-side integration tests for capability registration, task keepalive expiry extension, blocked-task input propagation, and task turns round-trip.

title: Rewrite hermes in Rust: translate run_agent.py into well-factored acc-hermes crate with live capability registration
description:
Why: hermes is a 4000-line single-file Python module that runs in a separate environment from where it is deployed. It has no module boundaries, no type safety, a fragile import chain (see: NameError incidents), and capability registration is static (set once at init, never updated). The goal is not a port of the Python — it is a ground-up redesign based on what hermes does, written in idiomatic Rust, that delivers the same behavior with proper module structure, compile-time safety, and live capability advertisement.

What hermes actually does (the behaviors to preserve and improve):
- Maintains a conversation loop with an LLM (Claude via Anthropic API or compatible)
- Executes tool calls returned by the LLM (shell, file read/write, web fetch, etc.)
- Claims work from the ACC queue, executes it, reports results back
- Registers with the hub and sends heartbeats including current capability set
- Handles multi-turn context windows and conversation summarization

Design direction for acc-hermes (Rust crate, lives in acc-agent or new top-level crate):
- Trait-based tool system: each tool is a struct implementing a Tool trait (name, schema, execute); tools are registered at startup and advertised in heartbeat
- Async-first: use tokio throughout; no blocking I/O on the async runtime
- Conversation engine: typed message history, token budget tracking, auto-summarization when approaching context limit
- LLM provider abstraction: trait covering Anthropic, OpenAI-compat, local (ollama); provider selected by config
- Queue integration: acc-hermes claims tasks from /api/queue using the same claiming protocol acc-agent uses today
- Capability advertisement: at heartbeat time, emit the live list of registered tools + LLM provider info so the DAG scheduler can route to the right agent
- Structured logging: tracing spans per task, exportable to the exec log
- Graceful shutdown: finish current tool call, post partial result, then exit cleanly on SIGTERM

Migration path:
- Run acc-hermes alongside Python hermes initially; route new tasks to Rust, keep Python for in-flight work
- Python hermes deprecated once acc-hermes passes the full tool integration test suite
- agent-pull.sh smoke test updated to validate acc-hermes binary rather than Python import
close_reason: Native Rust hermes rewrite complete: tool trait system, AnthropicProvider, conversation engine, agent loop with keepalive+SIGTERM, HERMES_BACKEND=python fallback. 133 tests passing.
